title: Modern JS (ES6-ES11)

questions.question_text: What does the spread operator (...) do for an array?
options: Creates a deep copy of the array
Spreads the elements of the array into a new context
questions.explanation: The spread operator expands the elements of an array (or object) into a new array or object.

options: A way to write HTML in JS
Using backticks (`) to allow multi-line strings and variable interpolation with ${}
A new way to write comments
questions.explanation: Template literals make string concatenation much cleaner and easier to read.

questions.question_text: What is the benefit of 'Arrow Functions'?
options: They provide a shorter syntax and have lexical 'this' binding
They automatically return values without the 'return' keyword
questions.explanation: Arrow functions are great for callbacks and don't rebind 'this' incorrectly.

options: Extracts values from arrays or properties from objects into distinct variables in one line
Changes the structure of the DOM
Breaks the code into smaller files
questions.explanation: obj = {x:1}; const {x} = obj; is much cleaner than const x = obj.x;.

options: It adds three dots to a string
It allows an iterable to be expanded in places where multiple arguments/elements are expected
It spreads the code across multiple files
It is a way to handle errors
questions.explanation: Use it to copy arrays, merge objects, or pass arrays as arguments.

questions.question_text: What is the purpose of the 'Rest Parameter' (...args)?
options: To collect multiple arguments into a single array inside a function
To stop a function from running
questions.explanation: It replaces the old 'arguments' object with a real array.

questions.question_text: Which array method creates a NEW array with the results of calling a function on every element?
questions.explanation: map() is fundamental for transforming data in modern frontend frameworks.

questions.question_text: Which array method returns only the elements that pass a specific condition?
questions.explanation: filter() always returns an array, even if only one item matches.

options: Breaking code into reusable files and managing dependencies
questions.explanation: Modern JS development uses ES Modules to keep codebases organized.

questions.question_text: What is 'Classes' in JS (introduced in ES6)?
options: Syntactic sugar over JS's existing prototype-based inheritance
questions.explanation: Classes make object-oriented programming in JS look more familiar to developers of other languages.

questions.question_text: Which ES6 feature allows you to set a value if an argument is missing?
What does the 'Optional Chaining' operator (?.) do?
options: Safely accesses nested object properties even if an intermediate property is null or undefined
questions.explanation: It prevents 'Cannot read property X of undefined' errors.

options: Checks if a value is false
Returns the right-hand side if the left-hand side is null or undefined (but NOT if it's 0 or '')
questions.explanation: It's safer than using || when you want to allow 0 or empty strings as valid values.

questions.question_text: What is the 'const' keyword used for?
options: Declaring a variable that can never be changed
Declaring a variable that cannot be reassigned (but its contents, like an object, can still be modified)
Making the code run on a server
questions.explanation: 90% of your variables in modern JS should be const.

questions.question_text: Which array method checks if at least ONE element in an array passes a test?

options: A function that returns a Promise and allows the use of 'await'
A function that doesn't have a name
A function used only for CSS
questions.explanation: Async/await is the modern way to handle asynchronous code in a linear, readable style.
